units.title: Fabric login procedure
units.reason: Section 6.2 defines a required login flow with explicit request, response branches, completion conditions, and relogin behavior for establishing Fabric operating characteristics before other Nx_Port communication.

evidence_refs.quote: Login with the Fabric is required for all Nx_Ports, regardless of the class supported.
The explicit Fabric Login procedure shall require an Nx_Port to transmit a Fabric Login (FLOGI) Link
The Nx_Port shall transmit the FLOGI in a new Exchange.
The following are possible responses the Nx_Port may receive when transmitting a FLOGI:
The Originator of the FLOGI request considers Fabric Login to have ended when
The Responder of the FLOGI request considers Fabric Login to have ended when
During a Login with the Fabric, if the Nx_Port was previously logged in with the Fabric and the

units.title: Fabric login response branches
units.reason: The explicit Fabric Login response section enumerates normal Fabric acceptance, point-to-point detection, busy, reject, retry, and self-connection branches, which are implementation-facing control-flow outcomes.

evidence_refs.quote: 6.2.2.3  Responses to Explicit Fabric Login
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI
b) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI
F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
e) F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
g) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the FLOGI.
h) No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.
If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name, then the Nx_Port is connected to it-

units.title: N_Port login procedure
units.reason: Sections 6.3.1-6.3.2 define explicit N_Port Login after Fabric Login, with separate operational flows depending on whether a Fabric is present.

evidence_refs.quote: N_Port Login follows the Fabric Login procedure.
The explicit N_Port Login procedure shall require an Nx_Port to transmit a PLOGI Link request Se-
The destination Nx_Port explicit Login procedure requires transmission of a N_Port Login (PLOGI)
6.3.2.4  N_Port Login - No Fabric present

units.title: N_Port login responses with Fabric present
units.reason: Section 6.3.2.3 enumerates implementation branches for PLOGI with a Fabric present, including accept, Fabric busy/reject, destination busy/reject, collision, LS_RJT, timeout, and self-connection outcomes.

evidence_refs.quote: 6.3.2.3  Responses to N_Port Login - Fabric present
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI
b) F_BSY with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
F_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
d) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
e) P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the N_Port_ID of receiving Nx_Port.
g) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
h) No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.

units.title: N_Port login responses with no Fabric present
units.reason: Section 6.3.2.5 gives the point-to-point response branches for PLOGI when no Fabric is present, including normal accept, busy/reject branches, collision handling, timeout, and self-connection handling.

evidence_refs.quote: 6.3.2.5  Responses to N_Port Login - No Fabric present
a) LS_ACC reply Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI
b) P_BSY Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
P_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
d) LS_RJT Sequence with OX_ID equal to the OX_ID of the PLOGI.
e) PLOGI Sequence.  The D_ID is the address identifier of receiving Nx_Port.
No Response may indicate a delivery error, e.g., error on the physical transport.
g) If the received N_Port_Name is equal to its N_Port_Name, then the Nx_Port is connected to it-

units.title: Process login procedure
units.reason: Section 7.1 defines PRLI as the explicit procedure for establishing an operating environment and image pairs after N_Port login, with retry/error behavior and separate PA relationship modes.

units.title: Process logout procedure
units.reason: Section 7.2 defines PRLO as the inverse of PRLI for invalidating an image-pair operating environment, freeing resources, resetting TYPE-specific parameters, and retrying after certain failures.

evidence_refs.quote: The Process Logout (PRLO) ELS request shall be used to request invalidation of the operating envi-
frees resources committed by a previous PRLI function.

units.title: Explicit logout procedure
units.reason: LOGO is the explicit logout mechanism used to remove service between Nx_Ports or between an Nx_Port and the Fabric, and it is also referenced as a PRLI prerequisite/error response when N_Port login is absent.

evidence_refs.quote: The LOGO ELS provides a method for explicitly removing service between two Nx_Port_IDs or be-
For an explicit Nx_Port Logout, the S_ID field designates the source Nx_Port_ID re-
explicit Fabric Logout originated by an Nx_Port, the S_ID field shall be the Nx_Port_ID to be logged
LS_ACC signifies that service has been removed for the N_Port_ID indicated in the pay-
